judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent correctly queried languages and commits and merged them, but in execute_python (function-call-2805671709898573738) it used a regex r'([A-Za-z]+)\s+\(([0-9,]+)\s+bytes\)' that fails to capture languages like "C++", causing tensorflow/tensorflow to be misclassified as Python and excluded, resulting in only four repositories returned instead of the correct five.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered repositories by applying language_description NOT LIKE '%Python%' (query_db on metadata_database.languages) and then merged with commit counts (from artifacts_database.commits), which misinterprets “main language not Python” and wrongly excluded repos whose descriptions mention Python anywhere, yielding only ["twbs/bootstrap"] instead of the top five.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent used the correct tables but, in execute_python, parsed languages.language_description with the regex r'(\w+) \((\d+) bytes\)', which fails on the comma-formatted numbers shown in the trace (e.g., "22,438 bytes"), causing incorrect main language extraction and leading to the omission of facebook/react and returning only four repositories instead of five.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent followed a correct plan but implemented language extraction with a naive regex in execute_python (call-743875733839229100) that failed to parse language names with symbols/spaces (e.g., "C++", "Objective-C"), causing key repos like apple/swift and facebook/react to be excluded from the non-Python list and leading the final filtered top list (call-483547884526604129) to return only three repositories instead of the correct five.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent ultimately used a filtered commits query (function-call-4121349776459893116) built from the languages table, but due to an implementation mistake it lowercased "Microsoft/vscode" in the IN clause (not matching the "Microsoft/vscode" values in commits) and included "torvalds/linux", leading it to return ["torvalds/linux","apple/swift","twbs/bootstrap","facebook/react","tensorflow/tensorflow"] instead of the ground-truth top five non-Python repos.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
